---
title: "Open and Closed Map"
author: "Youmin Chun"
date: "2026-07-10"
categories: 
  - Math > Topology
---

## Definition 
Let $(X, \mathscr{T}_X)$ and $(Y, \mathscr{T}_Y)$ be topological spaces, and let $f: X \to Y$ be a map.

**(i)** A map $f$ is called an ***open map*** if $f(U) \in \mathscr{T}_Y, \forall U \in \mathscr{T}_X$. 

**(ii)** A map $f$ is called a ***closed map*** if $f(C)$ is closed in $Y$ for each closed set $C$ in $X$.

## Theorem 1
The ***projections***  $\pi_1: X \times Y \to X$ and $\pi_2: X \times Y \to Y$ are open maps.

### Proof

## Theorem 2
A homeomorphism $f: X \to Y$ is an open map.

### Proof

## Remark
There is a continuous function but not open map, and there is an open map but not continuous. Thus, the continuous function and the open map are the independent concept.

# Reference
- James R. Munkres. (2000). Topology (2nd ed.). Pearson.
